← the Atlas

Remote terminals — the finale (R9–R10)

budding·proposed·JUN 28, 2026

The corrected R9–R10 plan. The awareness capability is ALREADY a decoupled library (@kolu/pulam-library — its dependency arrow points out, six consumers), and kolu already consumes it in-process. The earlier "kolu runs a local pulam process" correction (PR

Contents

  • Roadmap
  • What R9 reuses — and what it must build
  • The sub-phases
    • R9·lib + R9.0 — the shared per-terminal sensing leaf, driven by both homes — shipped
    • R9.0 — kolu drives the same leaf in-process · the decouple completes — shipped
    • R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped
    • R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile
    • R9.3 — remote awareness = mirror the remote pulam daemon
    • R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption
    • R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • The shape after R9.0
  • Open decisions
  • History

The last leg of #951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8 (#1594) shipped the right reader-join (a terminal is kolu.authored ⋈ terminalWorkspace.awareness, joined at the reader) — keep that. The question this note settles is how kolu gets the awareness half for a remote host, and the answer turns out to already be in the repo: the awareness capability is a decoupled library — @kolu/pulam-library — and kolu already consumes it in-process. The only real defect is that the library is a bag of pieces with no single sensing assembly, so the daemon and kolu each wire those pieces up themselves, divergently. This note corrects course: give the library one shared per-terminal sensing leaf (watchTerminalAwareness) and have both homes drive it — the pulam daemon by polling its kaval, kolu in-process from its own spawn/wake/adopt events (R9.0) — and mirror the remote pulam daemon for a remote host (R9.3). The decoupling is the library boundary, not a process boundary. The parent remote-terminals keeps the one-line rows; full model in the terminal model.

The earlier correction was itself a misdiagnosis — R9.0-as-a-process is CLOSED

The prior plan (and PR #1614) said R8’s mistake was that kolu serves awareness in-process, and proposed kolu run a local pulam process it mirrors over a socket. That was wrong on the diagnosis and the cure. Grounded in the code: @kolu/pulam-library already points its dependency arrow out (it names no kolu-app package; its lone host coupling is an injected logger) and already has six consumers — it is the decoupling. kolu already consumes it in-process (server/src/terminalEndpoint/local.ts runs the library’s startAwareness against kaval’s taps). So “decouple awareness from kolu in code” was solved before R9.0. The local-pulam-process bought no decoupling and introduced a fold-clobber blocker that is intrinsic to mirroring an ephemeral local process across a socket. PR #1614 is CLOSED (never merged); the process approach is rejected. The pulam daemon is kept for the remote case, where a process boundary is real.

The real gap — two assemblers, assembled differently

The library exposed the parts — the sensor engine (startAwareness) and the sink interface (AwarenessSink), the kaval-tap bridge (bridgeKavalTaps), the schema/seeds, the served-surface factory (serveTerminalWorkspace), the surface contract, the fs/git endpoint — but not the whole: “given a dialed kaval, produce a live, served terminalWorkspace surface.” Before R9·lib that assembly lived in the pulam daemon (runPulamDaemon), with two generic pieces stranded there — the per-terminal sink and the byte-tap activity tracker. R9·lib + R9.0 (below, shipped together) closed both halves: the per-terminal SENSING is now ONE shared leaf, watchTerminalAwareness (bridgeKavalTaps → cwd-persist → startAwareness → raw-output activity tap), and both homes drive it — the daemon by polling, kolu from its lifecycle events — so kolu’s divergent in-line subset inside local.ts is gone. Two assemblers became one leaf. (The first cut shipped a maximal createPulam assembly the daemon wrapped; the re-review found that the wrong boundary — it complected “sense one terminal”, genuinely shared, with each home’s discovery / seed / sink / served-collection, which are legitimately per-home — and replaced it with the leaf; see History.)

Roadmap

FINALE — R9 (corrected) · R10✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓R9·lib · the shared per-terminal sensing leaf (watchTerminalAwareness) + the library boundaryshipped · #1617
✓R9.0 · kolu drives the same leaf in-process — the decouple completesshipped · #1617
✓R9.1 · the TerminalEndpoint resolver (orthogonal — shipped)#1603
○R9.2 · ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tileneeds R9.1
○R9.3 · remote awareness = mirror the remote pulam daemonneeds R9.0 · R9.2 · R7
○R9.4 · persisted location + reconnect + adoptionneeds R9.2
○R9.5 · Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulseneeds R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3
○R10 · canvas multiplex + validateneeds R9.5

R9·lib + R9.0 are the new foundation — the library owns the one shared per-terminal sensing leaf, and each home owns its own discovery loop that drives it: the daemon polls, kolu fires on spawn/wake/adopt. The daemon and kolu stop re-deriving the sensing. R9.1 shipped (the PTY-endpoint path, orthogonal to awareness). R9.3 is symmetric to R9.0 — the same surface, mirrored over the wire instead of called in-process. The earlier optional “R9-converge” phase is dropped (it presupposed a local pulam socket for pulam-web to dial; there is none in the in-process model, and pulam-web already keeps its own pulam).

What R9 reuses — and what it must build

Shipped seams (reuse as-is):

seam where leg it serves
serveTerminalWorkspace @kolu/pulam-library the surface-skeleton factory both homes already call — the awareness + activity backing injection point
watchTerminalAwareness @kolu/pulam-library the ONE shared per-terminal sensing leaf both homes drive — the . entry, alongside the AwarenessRecord/AwarenessSink types + the composition pieces (makeAwarenessSink, createActivityTracker, liveActivity, seedAwarenessValue). Its sensing internals — startAwareness and the kaval-tap bridge bridgeKavalTaps — are NOT exported: the leaf is the only way to drive them, so a second hand-rolled assembler is unspellable (sharedLeaf.assembler.test.ts proves it)
the pulam daemon pulam (R4) the one sensor set per host; serves terminalWorkspaceSurface standalone — kept for the remote case
frontDaemonOverStdio @kolu/surface-daemon dial a remote kaval over ssh stdio → the remote PTY
getHostSession @kolu/surface-nix-host a pooled, long-lived session to dial a remote pulam → the mirror
mirrorRemoteSurface @kolu/surface the total dual ({ procedures, done }, R7) — mirror a remote pulam’s surface into one local handle
HostLocation remote arm · persisted location @kolu/common { kind:"remote", hostId }, round-trips to disk

Must build:

gap today the phase
one shared per-terminal sensing leaf (watchTerminalAwareness) + the sink and byte-tap moved into the library ✓ done — the sensing assembly + sink + tracker lived in the pulam daemon; kolu re-implemented a divergent subset R9·lib (shipped)
kolu drives the same leaf in-process (drop kolu’s hand-rolled assembly: inline channels + startAwareness call) ✓ done — kolu fires the leaf from spawn/wake/adopt; the daemon polls + leaf R9.0 (shipped)
the ssh driver (the remote PTY arm) only {kind:"local"} exists R9.2
mirror a remote pulam daemon’s surface as kolu’s remote awareness backing — R9.3

The sub-phases

R9·lib + R9.0 — the shared per-terminal sensing leaf, driven by both homes — shipped #1617

R9·lib· the shared sensing leaf (watchTerminalAwareness) + the library boundary✓ shipped
needs ← R6 · R4links #1617

The gap, grounded. The library (@kolu/pulam-library) is genuinely decoupled — its dependency arrow points out (package.json: @kolu/surface, kaval, the agent/git integrations; no kolu-app dependency) and it has six consumers. But it exported the sensing parts — the sensor engine startAwareness, the kaval-tap bridge bridgeKavalTaps, the per-terminal sink, the byte-tap tracker, the surface-skeleton factory serveTerminalWorkspace — never the one thing that wires them per terminal. So the pulam daemon assembled them one way (inside runPulamDaemon, with the sink + tracker stranded in the daemon package) and kolu assembled a divergent subset another way inside local.ts: its own inline four-channel build, its own startAwareness call, its own sink. Two assemblers, assembled differently — the root of the local-vs-remote divergence.

The shape that shipped — ONE shared sensing leaf, two per-home loops. The genuinely-shared volatility is “sense ONE terminal”: given a dialed kaval + an already-seeded record + an injected sink + a per-terminal activity hook, wire bridgeKavalTaps → cwd-persist → startAwareness → raw-output activity tap. That, and only that, is the library leaf:

watchTerminalAwareness({ kaval, id, record, sink, activity, log }) → stop()

It bakes in none of what is legitimately per-home — discovery, record-seeding, the sink implementation, the served collection, the activity-tracker ownership — so each home injects those and drives the leaf from its own loop:

concern the pulam daemon (awarenessLoop.ts) kolu-server (local.ts)
discovery polls kaval.terminal.list and reconciles fires on its own spawn / wake / adopt events; departure on the exit tap (kolu-only — the daemon has no exit tap)
seed seedAwarenessValue(cwd), published before the leaf runs the restored registry record (getTerminal(id).awareness) — never a fresh seed (the fold-clobber fix)
sink the plain library makeAwarenessSink (publish the whole value) its richer fold sink — the persisted/live terminals:dirty fence + trackRecent + screen-scrape — stays kolu-side
served collection a cache-backed store it owns the registry projection (the sink writes through workspaceSurfaceCtx)
activity tracker one per host; served live via liveActivity one per host on the endpoint; served live via localTerminalActivity

The leaf never re-seeds or over-publishes the record it is handed — it only reacts to taps through the injected sink — so a restored cwd / git / agentSession survives untouched on adopt/wake/restore. That is the exact defect that killed the first local-pulam-process cut, now structurally impossible.

Activity went live (R9.0), without double-sourcing the dot. kolu’s served terminalWorkspace.activity was a constant-empty quietActivity; now the leaf’s raw-output tap feeds kolu’s own per-host tracker, so the served stream is honest (localTerminalActivity). kolu’s client green dot stays the local useTerminalActivity — it has a resize/reveal suppress window the server tap structurally cannot replicate, and it lights instantly off the local xterm sink. The two are not a double-source: kolu’s client paints LOCAL tiles from useTerminalActivity; the served stream’s consumers are REMOTE viewers (pulam-web’s fleet board today, R9.3’s mirror tomorrow) — a disjoint set. Retiring the client path is a separate later refactor that must first solve server-side resize-suppression.

Design calls this phase settled (and why createPulam was dropped)

The first cut shipped a maximal createPulam(kaval) that owned the whole per-terminal loop (seed → sink → tap-bridge → sensors → activity tap → reconcile) and a two-step start(collection); the daemon wrapped it. The re-review rejected that boundary: its poll-reconcile loop complected “sense one terminal” (shared) with “discover terminals + seed + which sink + which collection” (per-home). kolu could not reuse it — its sleeping tiles would be poll-removed, its restored awareness re-seeded/clobbered, its fold sink baked out. So the shared unit is the leaf, not createPulam; createPulam and its two-step are gone. Each home owns its own discovery loop and injects the rest. The earlier “how much does createPulam own” / “where does the sink write” / “does activity go live” scope calls are all settled by this shape; the rename rode this phase too.

  • Needs: R6 (the library exists) and R4 (the pulam daemon) — both shipped.
  • Done: there is one shared per-terminal sensing leaf in the library (watchTerminalAwareness); its internals (startAwareness, bridgeKavalTaps) are not even on the export surface; the daemon polls + leaf (awarenessLoop.ts, no awareness logic of its own); kolu fires the same leaf from spawn/wake/adopt (local.ts), its inline channels + startAwareness call deleted, its fold sink kept and injected; kolu’s served activity is live; the reader-join + disk persistence are byte-unchanged. A structural guard (pulam-library/src/sharedLeaf.assembler.test.ts) walks both homes’ import graphs and rejects every form that reaches a sensing primitive (named — keyed off the imported symbol, so an alias can’t hide it — namespace, dynamic import()), over a denylist derived from the library so a new primitive can’t drift past — and asserts the differential the original R9·lib done-criterion wanted: daemon path == kolu path == the one watchTerminalAwareness leaf, neither re-rolling it. The daemon’s real-kaval daemon.test.ts e2e (serve correct awareness end-to-end, reconcile on kill) stays green.

R9.0 — kolu drives the same leaf in-process · the decouple completes — shipped #1617

R9.0· kolu drives the shared leaf in-process from its lifecycle events✓ shipped
needs ← R9·liblinks #1617

The point (shipped together with R9·lib above). kolu’s local awareness is now the same watchTerminalAwareness leaf the daemon drives, called in-process — no daemon, no socket, no mirror, no fold. kolu’s startAwarenessSensors deleted its inline tap-bridge and its startAwareness call and now just builds the record off its restored registry entry, builds its fold sink, and drives the leaf; the kolu-only exit tap (lifecycle, not sensing) stays beside it on its own abort. The reader-join is exactly as R8 shipped it: the browser subscribes to terminalWorkspace.awareness ⋈ kolu.authored and joins via composeTerminalMetadata. The arrow points into the library, in-process — not re-coupling, because the library knows nothing of kolu.

This is the in-process face of the same surface R9.3 mirrors remotely. It starts no second process and changes nothing the browser reads — only how kolu’s awareness collection is backed (the shared leaf, not a bespoke in-line wiring).

  • Migration: a hard cutover — one change, no flag and no fallback (the no-knobs rule).
  • Needs: R9·lib (same PR).
  • Done: kolu-server runs no bespoke sensing assembly — its local awareness is the shared leaf, driven from its own events; the inline channels + startAwareness call are gone, the fold sink kept; the reader-join + disk persistence are unchanged; the structural guard above pins kolu’s path == the daemon’s path == the one leaf (the differential the original criterion wanted, now that the second home is plugged in).

R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped #1603

R9.1· HostLocation-keyed endpoint resolver✓ shipped
needs ← R8links #1603

The PTY-endpoint path — orthogonal to awareness, so the correction above doesn’t touch it. resolveTerminalEndpoint is the one HostLocation-keyed seam mapping a terminal to its kaval endpoint; the {kind:"remote"} arm fails loudly until R9.2 fills it (.exhaustive() forces a deliberate arm). Per-terminal ops resolve off entry.meta.location, so R9.2 is a one-case addition.

R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile

R9.2· ssh kaval driver — remote PTY○ todo
needs ← R9.1

Add the {kind:"remote",hostId} arm to the resolver: dial the remote host’s kaval over frontDaemonOverStdio (the same ssh <host> kaval --stdio relay kaval-tui --host uses) → a remote PTY on the canvas, PTY only (no awareness badge yet). Persist location = {kind:"remote",hostId}. Adoption reuses the local boot’s adoptOrEnsure spine + the pid-gate’s atomic link(2) (a dial joins a host’s kaval, never races to kill it).

  • Needs: R9.1.
  • Done: dial a host, spawn a PTY, see an ssh-badged remote tile that echoes; it survives a network blip via re-attach. e2e: a remote PTY round-trips over ssh.

R9.3 — remote awareness = mirror the remote pulam daemon

R9.3· remote awareness — mirror the remote pulam daemon's surface○ todo
needs ← R9.0 · R9.2 · R7

A remote host runs the pulam daemon — which, after R9·lib + R9.0, drives the same shared watchTerminalAwareness leaf (poll + leaf) and serves the result over a socket. So kolu’s remote awareness backing is just a mirror of that daemon’s served surface, dialed over a long-lived getHostSession and mirrored via R7’s total dual. It is the wire-side face of the same surface kolu consumes in-process locally (R9.0): the contract (terminalWorkspaceSurface), the reader-join, the authored half, and disk persistence are identical — only the source differs (an in-process call vs a mirror over ssh). The remote tile gains the git/agent/PR badge + green dot; activity rides the mirror.

This is also where the genuinely remote-only reconciliation lives: a restored remote tile’s persisted awareness (git, lastActivityAt) must survive a fresh daemon re-deriving it across the wire. That fold belongs here, on the remote backing, not in the local in-process path (where there is no socket and no reseed).

  • Needs: R9.0 (the in-process consumer proves the surface), R9.2 (the remote PTY), R7 (the total dual).
  • Done: a remote tile shows live awareness mirrored from the remote pulam daemon, joined by the same reader-join as local; a differential test pins it equal to the remote pulam’s own awareness.

R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption

R9.4· persisted location + reconnect + adoption○ todo
needs ← R9.2

Harden the remote lifecycle: re-validate the persisted location on reconnect, and cover the adoption/reconcile cases.

the remote kaval is… kolu does
absent provision + spawn fresh (adoptOrEnsure → ensure)
live · wire-compatible adopt — connect, never kill; its PTYs reconcile in
live · a build behind adopt + an “update pending” nudge
live · contract-skewed recycle (kill → respawn) — only on a typed DaemonContractSkewError
unreachable bounded retry, then degraded — never killed
  • Open (decide here): what is re-validated on reconnect and the fail behavior. location is the host discriminator (kolu.authored); cwd is the observed dir (terminalWorkspace.awareness) — different collections. Spell out the comparison and the fail-fast (crash loudly vs re-derive); don’t invent it at implementation time.
  • Needs: R9.2.
  • Done: a kolu-server restart re-adopts remote PTYs; each adoption-table row has a test.

R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)

R9.5· Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulse○ todo
needs ← R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3

The one large client change, and the only finale phase R-pulamweb-4 gates. The Code tab reads koluSurface’s value-bearing streams today (CodeTab.tsx — app.streams.gitStatus/fsListAll/gitDiff.use). Rewrite it to the shared surface’s procedure + {seq} pulse: call the procedure once, re-query on each pulse (the two wire shapes are in surface live data). This deletes the last koluSurface fs/git reads and works for local and remote tiles alike.

  • Needs: R-pulamweb-4 (proves the procedure + pulse remote leg over websocketLink + surfaceClient), R9.3.
  • Done: the Code tab shows live git status/diff for a remote tile via procedure + pulse; no koluSurface fs/git reads remain.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← R9.5

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front.

  • Needs: R9 (all of it).
  • Done: the validated UX call, recorded.

The shape after R9.0

Open decisions

Resolve each before the phase that needs it — so the implementer isn’t choosing mid-flight.

decision needed by options
reconnect re-validation semantics R9.4 what’s compared (location vs the remote kaval’s live cwd) + the fail-fast behavior

The R9·lib/R9.0 design calls — the shared unit (the leaf, not createPulam), where the in-process sink writes (kolu’s registry, injected), and kolu’s activity source (served live + the client useTerminalActivity kept) — are settled by the shipped shape above; see History.

History

  • 2026-06 · R9·lib + R9.0 — createPulam → the shared leaf #1617. The first cut of R9·lib lifted the per-terminal assembly into a maximal createPulam(kaval) the daemon wrapped, returning { served, start(collection) } with a built-in poll-reconcile loop. The perfection re-review found that the wrong boundary: createPulam’s loop complected “sense one terminal” (genuinely shared) with “discover terminals + seed the record + which sink + which served collection” (legitimately per-home — the daemon observes by polling; kolu owns via spawn/sleep/wake/adopt). kolu could not reuse it without its sleeping tiles being poll-removed and its restored awareness re-seeded. So the maximal assembly was replaced by the per-terminal leaf watchTerminalAwareness, and R9·lib + R9.0 were finished together: each home owns its discovery loop (the daemon polls in awarenessLoop.ts; kolu fires from its lifecycle events in local.ts) and injects the seed/sink/collection/tracker, while the leaf owns only the sensing. createPulam + its two-step start() were deleted; the structural guard generalized to assert both homes rest on the leaf (the differential test the original criterion wanted, now that kolu is the second home).

Referenced by

  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • The Terminal Model — two collections, one reader-side join (and what to name them)
